The predicate `(Nat.digits 10 m).length = n` combined with `m < 10^n` correctly characterizes n-digit base-10 numbers (Nat.digits is in little-endian with no trailing zeros, so length equals the digit count for m ≥ 1). The conjunction of `Even (count 1)`, `count 1 ≠ 0`, `Even (count 3)`, `count 3 ≠ 0` precisely matches "1 and 3 each occur a nonzero, even number of times". No hypotheses are added or dropped beyond what the informal claim requires.
